sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
after = last_transition.after_frame

print("Current key objects:")
seg = after.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    c = n['color']
    p = n['pixels']
    if 4 <= p <= 50:
        b = n['boundary']
        center_r = sum([pt[0] for pt in b]) // len(b)
        center_c = sum([pt[1] for pt in b]) // len(b)
        print(f"  {c} p={p} ({center_r},{center_c}) h={n['hash'][:8]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting changes:
- O moved from (12,32) to (10,24) - moved back along the path
- W appeared at (12,32) - where orange was
- The w path objects disappeared
- G(12,24) changed to G(13,24) with only 4 pixels (was 12)
- W(18,57) disappeared
- G(6,17) still there
So clicking orange moved it back along the path and left a white diamond behind. The path objects disappeared. This is like the orange square is moving backwards along the path.
Let me think about the pattern:
1. Click purple ring → creates path from orange to ring, orange moves to (12,32), white diamond at ring center
2. Click orange at (12,32) → orange moves back to (10,24), white diamond at (12,32)
So clicking orange moves it along the path. Let me try clicking orange again to see if it continues moving.
Wait, but I need to understand the direction. The path goes from G(6,17) → G(12,24) → w(14,26) → O(19,30) → S(23,35) → purple ring.
After clicking purple ring, orange moved from (19,30) to (12,32) which is further along the path toward the ring.
After clicking orange at (12,32), it moved back to (10,24) which is closer to G(12,24).
So it seems like clicking orange moves it in alternating directions or something. Let me try clicking it again.
